title: Crassocephalum manampanihense
taxonomy: Crassocephalum manampanihense is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Crassocephalum, which is a group of plants commonly known as broad-leaved or large-leaved African daisies. The species is native to Madagascar and is part of the larger group of plants known as flowering plants or angiosperms. Crassocephalum manampanihense is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification includes the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae, and the genus Crassocephalum.

intro: Crassocephalum manampanihense, also known as Manampampi, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Crassocephalum manampanihense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Madagascan medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are administered to patients to alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
